free_text: Recoil Study of Transfer Reactions. Evidence For Alpha
And be-8 Transfers in C-12 Reactions with Heavy
Nuclei (AU, BI)
Institut De Physique Nucleaire, Orsay
. Alice Accelerator
Typical Intensity were 0.1 to 0.5 Muamperes,
And the Bombardments Lasted 4-9 Hours.
The Foil Stack was Placed in a Faraday Cup and was
Bombarded Simultaneously with Two Thin Targets Placed
In Two Measuring Chambers Designed to Measure Angular
Distributions and Recoil Ranges, Respectively.
The Energy Degradation in the Stack was Calculated
According to L.C.Northcliffe Et Al. Nucl. Data Tables
The Energy of the Primary Beam was Calculated from
Acceleration Frequency and Extraction Radius. These
Calculated Values were Checked in Several Cases by a
Direct Energy Measurement with a Solid-State Detector
And Agreement to Within 1% was Found.
The Incident Beam Energy Could be Varied Between 60
And 90 MeV.
Stack Consisting of Foils of 1 Mg/Cm**2 Au Deposited on
1 Mg/Cm**2 Al. The Al Backing was Placed Downstream
And was Thick Enough to Stop the Recoil Nuclei.
Lakw
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Some Corrections For 'Reaction'.
Converted to Lower Case.  Angular
Distributions are Included.
V.Mc-Lane Remark For B0126006 is Included
Last checking has been done.
Due to Selfabsorption the Alpha-Counting Efficiency was
< 50%.  This Effect had to be Corrected For Using the
Simultaneously Measured Recoil Ranges and Angular
Distributions.
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors: Counter Efficiencies (+-1% FOR
ALPHA RAYS), Target Thickness (+-5%), Beam Intensity
(+-5%), Activity Measurement (VARIABLE).
- By Compiler of CAJAD -
Cumulative Production Via (C-12,2N)-Compound Nuclear
Reaction and Subsequent Fast Alpha-Decay in Addition
To the Direct Transfer is Possible Too.
Cumulative Production Via (C-12,3N)-Compound Nuclear
Reaction and Subsequent Fast Alpha-Decay in Addition
To the Direct Transfer is Assumed by the Authors.
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors:  Counter Efficiencies (+-5% FOR
GAMMA RAYS), Target Thickness (+-5%), Beam Intensity
(+-5%), Activity Measurement (VARIABLE).
The Authors Assume Mainly Cumulative Production Via
(C-12,4N)-Reaction and Subsequent Fast Alpha-Decay.
Due to Selfabsorption the Alpha-Counting Efficiency was
<50%.  This Effect had to be Corrected For Using the
Simultaneously Measured Recoil Ranges and Angular
Distributions.
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors:  Counter Efficiencies (+-5% FOR
GAMMA RAYS, +-1% FOR ALPHA RAYS), Branching Ratios (5
TO 10% IN CASES OF GAMMA INTENSITIES <1), Target
Thickness (+-5%), Beam Intensity (+-5%), Activity
Measurement (VARIABLE).
55% Isomeric
Transition - Comment by the Compiler of Kachapag -
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors:  Counter Efficiencies (+-5% FOR
GAMMA RAYS), Branching Ratios (5 TO 10% IN CASES OF
GAMMA INTENSITIES <1), Target Thickness (+-5%), Beam
Intensity (+-5%), Activity Measurement (VARIABLE).
An Additional Error of +-8% has to be Added Due to the
Following Reason. The Relative Production of the Two
Isomers is Unknown. However, the Extrapolation of the
5.3 Hr Asymptote of the Decay Curve was Used to
Calculate an Approximate Value of the Total Cross
Section For the Sum of Both Isomers.
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors:  Counter Efficiencies (+-5% FOR
GAMMA RAYS), Branching Ratios (5 TO 10% IN CASES OF
GAMMA INTENSITIES <1), Target Thickness (+-5%), Beam
Intensity (+-5%), Activity Measurement (VARIABLE).
Half-Life 21.5 Hr  - Comment by the
Compiler of Kachapag -
The Decay Curve Included the Accumulation of Pb-200.
Both Contributions Could be Derived from the Analysis
Of the Curve. Direct Production of Tl-200 was
Attributed to Transfer Reactions and Accumulation from
Pb-200 to a (C-12,5N)-Reaction Producing at-204 Which
Decayed to Tl-200 Via Bi-200 and Pb-200.
The Errors Given are the Square Roots of the
Quadratic Sums of the Individual Errors. Included are
The Following Errors:  Counter Efficiencies (+-5% FOR
GAMMA RAYS), Branching Ratios (5 TO 10% IN CASES OF
GAMMA INTENSITIES <1), Target Thickness (+-5%), Beam
Intensity (+-5%), Activity Measurement (VARIABLE).
Cross Sections were Calculated from Tl-201 Activity.
Obviously, Cumulative Cross Section of Tl-201
Production was Measured.
